PYPL.name: PayPal Holdings
PYPL.thesis: RSI=24 extreme oversold with Tencent/WeChat regulatory approval confirmed as near-term catalyst unlocking Chinese market access, driving 20.7% upside to $53 target. Extreme Fear environments historically precede sharp reversals in oversold payments names.

WMT.name: WMT
WMT.thesis: Bearish news flag DISMISSED — Jim Cramer bullish, strong-buy analyst consensus intact, discount-retail defensiveness rewarded in Extreme Fear environments; RSI=30 at oversold support with 16.1% upside. +0.3% 1-day holding ground during fear selloff confirms thesis.

NEM.name: Newmont
NEM.thesis: Highest screened score at 59 with RSI=40 neutral and bullish news flow; gold miners provide a direct portfolio hedge against deepening Extreme Fear with 34.9% upside. The -2.6% 1-day move is macro noise during a fear cascade — gold thesis strengthens as F&G declines.

PLTR.name: PLTR
thesis_invalidators: New catalyst or thesis break not yet reassessed

REGN.name: Regeneron Pharmaceuticals Inc.
REGN.thesis: Dupixent franchise durability; RSI=23 extreme oversold suggests contrarian entry potential once institutional selling stabilizes

PEP.name: PEP
PEP.thesis: RSI=32 oversold in a highly defensive staple with 16.1% upside; PepsiCo diversified snack and beverage portfolio provides recession-resistant revenue. Completes staples pair with WMT at 2-group maximum for maximum defensive allocation.

TSM.name: TSM
TSM.thesis: Taiwan Semiconductor re-enters screen with score 54 and RSI=52, offering neutral technicals with bullish AI chip demand tailwind as Intel/AMD comparative analysis drives wafer order upgrades. Correlation engine now ranks TSM over AVGO as preferred semiconductor pair alongside NVDA; geopolitical risk is known and priced.

GILD.name: GILD
GILD.thesis: Healthcare defensive with RSI=51 neutral and pending FDA milestone as near-term binary catalyst; Gilead HIV and oncology pipeline provides stable recurring revenue with upside optionality. 17.4% upside with low defensive risk appropriate for Extreme Fear regime.

AVGO.name: Broadcom
AVGO.thesis: HOLD existing position only — no new capital as correlation engine today selects NVDA+TSM as preferred semiconductor pair, displacing AVGO from active add slot. 14.2% upside to $480 remains but -1.6% 1-day underperformance and correlation displacement warrant zero new adds.

MA.name: Mastercard
MA.thesis: Mastercard global payments network provides structural growth through electronic payment adoption with RSI=42 offering technical room to run toward $647 target. Pairs with PYPL in payments group for balanced fintech exposure at 2-group maximum.

XOM.name: ExxonMobil
XOM.thesis: Residual TRIM — Altman Z=1.52 financial distress warrants continued reduction despite +0.9% 1-day. COP replaced XOM as primary energy holding; reduce to 0% or sub-0.5% residual on intraday strength above $148.50.

CRM.name: Salesforce
CRM.thesis: Minimal 1% position HOLD pending hard stop resolution at $160; -23.9% portfolio PnL is dominant risk with stop not yet triggered at current $180.53. Agentforce AI integration provides speculative upside but does not justify any capital addition.

AMZN.name: Amazon
AMZN.thesis: AWS cloud growth and advertising revenue diversification provide dual growth engines supporting 11.4% upside; +0.4% 1-day confirms stable demand thesis. Completes big_tech pair at 2-group maximum.

DIS.name: DIS
DIS.thesis: RSI=36 mild oversold with strong-buy consensus targeting $129; Disney+ streaming profitability milestone and parks demand resilience support 24.2% upside. German regulatory risk manageable and already reflected in neutral news sentiment.

FTNT.name: FTNT
FTNT.thesis: MANDATORY TRIM — RSI=87 Day 3 extreme overbought with +138.2% portfolio gain makes this highest-priority profit crystallization. Execute 40-50% position reduction at market today; each additional overbought day increases mean-reversion probability exponentially.

GOOGL.name: Alphabet
GOOGL.thesis: Big tech anchor with stable thesis supported by Cloud and Search monetization with incremental AI integration revenue. HOLD maintains allocation without adding risk in Extreme Fear regime; +0.1% 1-day confirms thesis intact.

COP.name: ConocoPhillips
COP.thesis: ConocoPhillips entered as cleaner energy replacement with superior Altman Z-score versus XOM; upstream-only model limits refining margin volatility with 12.9% upside. HOLD as primary energy position completing energy group alongside residual XOM trim.
